quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika, the country's most famous spice, comes in eight different varieties ranging from delicate and sweet to fiery hot. The pepper plants were brought to Hungary by the Turks in the 16th century, and Hungary now produces some of the world's finest paprika, particularly from the town of Szeged and Kalocsa.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Pacific Northwest
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals in the Strait of Juan de Fuca have unique personalities and form lasting friendships with each other. Scientists have discovered these intelligent marine mammals recognize individual seals even after years apart.

medium_read.title: How Plants Communicate Through Underground Networks
medium_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that plants use vast underground fungal networks to share nutrients and even warn each other of danger. This "wood wide web" shows that gardens are far more connected and intelligent than we ever imagined.

long_read.title: The Golden Age of Hungarian Thermal Baths
long_read.teaser: Hungary sits on a geological treasure trove of thermal springs, and for centuries these healing waters have been central to Hungarian culture and daily life. From Roman times to the Ottoman era to today's grand bath houses, this is the story of a nation shaped by water.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagles Soaring Over Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: July is a wonderful time to spot bald eagles at Ediz Hook, the natural sand spit that protects Port Angeles Harbor. These magnificent birds often perch on driftwood or soar above the water hunting for fish, and the spit's three-mile trail offers stunning views of both the eagles and the Strait of Juan de Fuca.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's exactly right! Their tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power those rapid wing beats.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These amazing little birds have hearts that beat about 1,200 times per minute — that's what powers their incredible hovering ability!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Anna's hummingbirds, common in Port Angeles, can beat their wings up to 80 times per second during courtship dives. Their hearts are proportionally the largest of all birds, making up about 2.5% of their body weight.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'hello' or 'goodbye'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Szia! You got it right! It's one of the most useful and friendly words in Hungarian.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'Szia' (pronounced 'see-ah'). It's an informal, warm way to say both hello and goodbye to friends!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Szia comes from the verb 'szolgál' meaning 'to serve,' originally from the phrase 'your humble servant.' For a more formal greeting, Hungarians say 'Jó napot' (good day).

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is the most common in Olympic National Forest?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Western Hemlock is indeed the most abundant tree in the Olympic forests. These beautiful evergreens thrive in the moist coastal climate and can live for over 500 years!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The answer is Western Hemlock! These graceful trees with their drooping tops are the most common in Olympic National Forest, perfectly adapted to our rainy climate.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Western Hemlock can grow up to 200 feet tall and are shade-tolerant, meaning they can grow beneath the canopy of other trees. Their small cones and delicate needles make them easy to identify on trails around Port Angeles.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Trees of Olympic — National Park Service

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
